message:
fix(security): crypto passphrase leak, SQL identifier escaping, Unicode path folding

Three security fixes from deep codebase audit:

1. crypto.rs: Don't include stderr in error messages when passphrase
   decryption fails — stderr could leak passphrase-related diagnostics
   to log files or error handlers.

2. finalize.rs: Use bracket-escaping [trigger_name] for SQL identifiers
   in DROP TRIGGER statements. Current trigger names are hardcoded
   constants (safe), but the pattern is dangerous if copied to dynamic
   names.

3. guard/lib.rs: Use Unicode-aware to_lowercase() instead of
   to_ascii_lowercase() for path normalization in case-insensitive
   mode. Ensures non-ASCII path components (agent names with accented
   characters) match correctly on case-insensitive filesystems
   (macOS HFS+, Windows NTFS).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
